prompt_rules: eight-frame walk cycle for ONE direction only, arranged in a 4x2 grid: TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right
high-resolution 16-bit-plus pixel art, each frame about 256x256, character drawn LARGE filling most of the cell height with a small margin
same character identity, facing and scale in every cell

prompt:
Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER WALK CYCLE sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing UP (back view: the back of his head, the tiny low ponytail with its vermilion cord, and the back of the indigo haori are visible; no face). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions of the attached reference character EXACTLY: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build with a solid chest, thick limbs and a broad stable stance - NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference. The back view must keep EXACTLY the same head size, same total height and same sturdy body width as the front view. The sword stays sheathed at his hip in every frame. 8-frame WALK CYCLE with proper alternating footsteps: frame1 = CONTACT A (his LEFT foot planted forward, right foot back, arms counter-swing), frame2 = weight drop (body 2-3px lower), frame3 = PASSING (feet together under the body), frame4 = push-off (body 2-3px higher), frame5 = CONTACT B (his RIGHT foot planted forward - the MIRROR step of frame1), frame6 = weight drop, frame7 = PASSING, frame8 = push-off. The forward foot MUST alternate between frame1 and frame5. Arms swing opposite to the legs. The soles of the lifted feet read clearly in back view. The haori back panel and sash knot sway with each step. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell, head at the same height in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
